data.body: PREPLANNING_CONTEXT: {"version":1,"epicId":"0b7c-d811","generatedAt":"2026-04-12T00:00:00Z","generatedBy":"preplanning","epic":{"title":"Add searchable UI/UX design knowledge base as a reference library","description":"Agents running design-review, ui-designer, brainstorm, preplanning, and sprint workflows currently operate without a shared corpus of UI/UX design knowledge. This epic establishes the foundational reference library \u2014 schema, corpus structure, full initial content, retrieval infrastructure, and source provenance tracking \u2014 that five subsequent epics will integrate into specific agent workflows.","successCriteria":["SC1: plugins/dso/data/ui-reference/ with domain-partitioned Markdown files, YAML frontmatter (id/title/domain[]/component[]/action[]/compliance[]/severity/story_type[]/coverage/source/license), three resolution tiers (Summary/Detail/Implementation). _schema.yaml + check-corpus-schema.sh pre-commit hook enforce tag vocabulary.","SC2: _index.yaml committed to repo (not generated), listing every entry with six-dimension tags, one-liner, file path, coverage tier, source, license.","SC3: plugins/dso/scripts/ref-query.sh \u2014 stdlib Python BM25; bm25s optional accelerator; --top-n (default 8), --tier, --session-hash; zero-result stderr signal; 10s target; 500-line output limit.","SC4: Full corpus: 48 gov anti-patterns (17de-a47a), USWDS, WCAG 2.2 AAA (Section 508/OMB M-24-08), Nielsen, Norman, GOV.UK, 18F UX Guide, digital.gov, Inclusive Design, cognitive load, Brignull dark patterns, AidUI, principles.design. Summary+Detail required; Implementation stretch.","SC5: Integration tests at tests/integration/test-ref-library-integration.sh (REPO_ROOT paths): (a) session-timeout query; (b) tier filtering; (c) manifest integrity; (d) 500-line limit; (e) precision sub-test \u2014 3 queries with expected-tag mapping; (f) 10s timing.","SC6: ref-query.sh registered as dso ref-query shim.","SC7: docs/ui-reference-sources.yaml tracking external sources (name, URL, type, pinned version/commit, last-synced, license, domains)."]},"stories":[{"id":"9fee-6947","title":"As a DSO plugin developer, I can query the UI/UX reference corpus through a retrieval script and get relevant tagged entries back","description":"Creates corpus directory, _schema.yaml, check-corpus-schema.sh (registered in .pre-commit-config.yaml), boundary allowlist entry, _index.yaml, ref-query.sh (stdlib BM25, all SC3 flags, 500-line limit), dso ref-query shim, docs/ui-reference-sources.yaml, seed entries","priority":1,"classification":"new","walkingSkeleton":true,"hasWireframe":false,"doneDefinitions":["plugins/dso/data/ui-reference/ exists with _schema.yaml (all six tag dimensions + recommended values); \u22653 domain subdirectories with valid seed entries across all three tiers","check-corpus-schema.sh registered in .pre-commit-config.yaml; passes on valid entries, fails with offending field on invalid","plugins/dso/data/** added to .claude/hooks/pre-commit/plugin-boundary-allowlist.conf; corpus commits not rejected","_index.yaml committed with all seed entries; --validate-index confirms integrity","ref-query.sh returns entries in \u226410s; 500-line output limit with stderr warning; zero-result stderr signal","dso ref-query shim works; check-shim-refs.sh passes","docs/ui-reference-sources.yaml committed with all planned sources","Unit tests pass (BM25 mock fixture, schema validation, manifest integrity, shim invocation)"],"considerations":["[Performance] BM25 re-reads corpus on each call \u2014 10s target must be verified at full corpus scale","[Reliability] check-corpus-schema.sh must fail-open gracefully if Python unavailable","[Security] ref-query.sh query string must not be shell-unquoted \u2014 use Python argument parsing","[Testing] BM25 unit tests must use mock corpus fixture","[Reliability] ref-query.sh must emit clear stderr error when python3 unavailable"],"scopeSplitCandidate":false,"splitRole":null,"splitPairId":null,"blockedBy":[],"satisfiesCriterion":"SC1, SC2, SC3, SC6, SC7"},{"id":"f5bd-27a9","title":"As a DSO agent running design review, I can query 48 government UI/UX anti-patterns to identify potential design problems in the work being reviewed","description":"All 48 anti-patterns from epic 17de-a47a formatted as Markdown + YAML frontmatter under anti-patterns/; domain tags anchored (auth/forms/navigation/visual); _index.yaml manually updated","priority":1,"classification":"new","walkingSkeleton":false,"hasWireframe":false,"doneDefinitions":["48 entries committed; valid frontmatter; domain tags anchored (session/auth \u2192 domain: [auth], forms \u2192 domain: [forms], navigation \u2192 domain: [navigation], visual \u2192 domain: [visual])","_index.yaml manually updated; check-corpus-schema.sh passes","dso ref-query 'session timeout' returns entry with domain: [auth]"],"considerations":["[Maintainability] Entry IDs must use prefix ap- for uniqueness across corpus","[Reliability] Add comment to 17de-a47a ticket after completion; close as superseded on epic completion"],"scopeSplitCandidate":false,"splitRole":null,"splitPairId":null,"blockedBy":["9fee-6947"],"satisfiesCriterion":"SC4 (government anti-patterns)"},{"id":"fab0-d0e2","title":"As a DSO agent running ui-designer, I can query USWDS component and design token guidance to inform component recommendations for federal web applications","description":"USWDS v3 components in components/ with domain: [components], license: CC0-1.0; form entries tagged component: [form]; _index.yaml manually updated","priority":1,"classification":"new","walkingSkeleton":false,"hasWireframe":false,"doneDefinitions":["USWDS v3 components (form inputs, buttons, nav, alerts, tables, etc.) committed; form entries use component: [form]","_index.yaml manually updated; check-corpus-schema.sh passes","dso ref-query 'USWDS form validation' returns result with component: [form]"],"considerations":["[Maintainability] Entries reference USWDS v3.x specifically; note version in source field","[Reliability] All USWDS entries must use license: CC0-1.0"],"scopeSplitCandidate":false,"splitRole":null,"splitPairId":null,"blockedBy":["9fee-6947"],"satisfiesCriterion":"SC4 (USWDS component and token guidance)"},{"id":"5a09-615c","title":"As a DSO agent running design review, I can query WCAG 2.2 AAA patterns cross-referenced to Section 508 to generate actionable accessibility guidance","description":"accessibility/ with all four WCAG 2.2 principles at AAA level; keyboard nav entries tagged action: [keyboard-nav] and compliance: [wcag-2.2-aaa]; Detail tier quotes normative SC text","priority":2,"classification":"new","walkingSkeleton":false,"hasWireframe":false,"doneDefinitions":["All four WCAG 2.2 principles covered with AAA-level SCs; keyboard entries use action: [keyboard-nav] (documented as tag contract)","_index.yaml manually updated; check-corpus-schema.sh passes","dso ref-query 'WCAG 2.2 keyboard navigation' returns result with compliance: [wcag-2.2-aaa] AND action: [keyboard-nav]"],"considerations":["[Accessibility] Detail tier must quote normative WCAG 2.2 SC text verbatim","[Reliability] Verify acceptable license for WCAG 2.2 content in corpus (W3C-Document)"],"scopeSplitCandidate":false,"splitRole":null,"splitPairId":null,"blockedBy":["9fee-6947"],"satisfiesCriterion":"SC4 (WCAG 2.2 AAA patterns)"},{"id":"6c26-4321","title":"As a DSO agent running brainstorm or preplanning, I can query core design heuristics and federal UX principles to ground design decisions in expert guidance","description":"principles/ \u2014 Nielsen 10 heuristics, Norman principles, GOV.UK 10 principles, 18F UX Guide, digital.gov federal requirements, Inclusive Design Principles; Summary+Detail required","priority":2,"classification":"new","walkingSkeleton":false,"hasWireframe":false,"doneDefinitions":["principles/ covers all 10 Nielsen heuristics, Norman key principles, GOV.UK 10 principles, 18F UX Guide, digital.gov federal requirements, Inclusive Design Principles; Summary+Detail tiers","_index.yaml manually updated; check-corpus-schema.sh passes","dso ref-query 'design heuristics' returns at least one entry from principles/"],"considerations":["[Maintainability] Multiple source licenses (OGL v3, public domain, CC-BY) \u2014 verify each and record in docs/ui-reference-sources.yaml","[Reliability] Nielsen material may require paraphrase rather than verbatim copy"],"scopeSplitCandidate":true,"splitRole":"foundation","splitPairId":"c642-4840","blockedBy":["9fee-6947"],"satisfiesCriterion":"SC4 (Nielsen, Norman, GOV.UK, 18F, digital.gov, Inclusive Design)"},{"id":"c642-4840","title":"As a DSO agent running brainstorm or preplanning, I can query dark pattern taxonomies, cognitive load checklists, and design principle catalogs to identify harmful patterns and improve design quality","description":"patterns/ \u2014 Brignull dark patterns, AidUI 27-class taxonomy, cognitive load + defensive design checklists, principles.design representative entries; Enhancement of Story-5a","priority":3,"classification":"new","walkingSkeleton":false,"hasWireframe":false,"doneDefinitions":["patterns/ covers Brignull dark patterns (12+), AidUI 27-class taxonomy, cognitive load/defensive design, principles.design representative entries; Summary+Detail tiers","_index.yaml manually updated; check-corpus-schema.sh passes","dso ref-query 'dark patterns' returns entry from Brignull taxonomy with domain: [patterns]"],"considerations":["[Maintainability] principles.design is MIT; use representative entries, not full catalog","[Reliability] Brignull dark patterns on deceptive.design \u2014 verify license; paraphrase with attribution if verbatim not permitted"],"scopeSplitCandidate":true,"splitRole":"enhancement","splitPairId":"6c26-4321","blockedBy":["9fee-6947","6c26-4321"],"satisfiesCriterion":"SC4 (Brignull, AidUI, cognitive load, defensive design, principles.design)"},{"id":"cfa6-6c7f","title":"As a DSO maintainer, I can run an automated integration test suite to verify the reference library's integrity, retrieval accuracy, and performance","description":"tests/integration/test-ref-library-integration.sh (named for runner discovery); all 6 sub-tests; REPO_ROOT paths; precision queries with anchored tag expectations","priority":1,"classification":"new","walkingSkeleton":false,"hasWireframe":false,"doneDefinitions":["test-ref-library-integration.sh exists, is executable; all 6 sub-tests pass including precision sub-test (a: domain:[auth], e: component:[form], wcag:[wcag-2.2-aaa]+[keyboard-nav], auth:[auth]); (f) completes in \u226410s","REPO_ROOT-based paths; passes from any worktree","Discovered by test-*-integration.sh runner glob"],"considerations":["[Testing] Precision sub-test expected tags must match tags declared in Stories 2-4 done definitions","[Reliability] Sub-test (f) timing depends on full corpus size; investigate BM25 loading if exceeded"],"scopeSplitCandidate":false,"splitRole":null,"splitPairId":null,"blockedBy":["9fee-6947","f5bd-27a9","fab0-d0e2","5a09-615c","6c26-4321","c642-4840"],"satisfiesCriterion":"SC5"},{"id":"5dcc-7811","title":"Update project docs to reflect UI/UX reference library infrastructure","description":"CLAUDE.md Architecture section (corpus, ref-query.sh, shim, hook, boundary allowlist maintenance); Quick Reference table (dso ref-query); KNOWN-ISSUES.md (_index.yaml regeneration reminder)","priority":3,"classification":"new","walkingSkeleton":false,"hasWireframe":false,"doneDefinitions":["CLAUDE.md Architecture references plugins/dso/data/ui-reference/, ref-query.sh, dso ref-query shim, check-corpus-schema.sh, docs/ui-reference-sources.yaml, and boundary allowlist maintenance requirement","CLAUDE.md Quick Reference includes dso ref-query with flags","KNOWN-ISSUES.md has entry noting _index.yaml must be manually updated after adding corpus domain files"],"considerations":["[Maintainability] Follow .claude/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md"],"scopeSplitCandidate":false,"splitRole":null,"splitPairId":null,"blockedBy":["9fee-6947","f5bd-27a9","fab0-d0e2","5a09-615c","6c26-4321","c642-4840","cfa6-6c7f"],"satisfiesCriterion":"Documentation update"}],"storyDashboard":{"totalStories":8,"uiStories":0,"criticalPath":["9fee-6947","f5bd-27a9","fab0-d0e2","5a09-615c","6c26-4321","c642-4840","cfa6-6c7f","5dcc-7811"]}}
